summary: Iteration 6 — backend regression on the 6 rewritten Crystal-Reports-style PDF endpoints + frontend regression after AvocatSheet split into 5 sub-components. Backend: 13/13 pytest cases pass (every PDF endpoint returns 401 unauthenticated, and 200 + valid %PDF signature when authenticated, with seeded mandat/mega/inhab data so PDFs are non-empty). Frontend: Sheet-stay-open behavior verified post-refactor for create, identification update, and adresses save; remaining sub-tab paths blocked by Playwright click quirks but the underlying logic (saveMega/saveInhab/saveWebTab/saveWebPassword all call onSaved({close:false})) is identical to iteration_5 which already validated all 8 acceptance criteria.
critical_code_review_comments: AvocatSheet.jsx is now ~232 lines after the split into /components/avocat/* — clean, well below the 700-line threshold. Good refactor.
useEffect split (form-sync on [avocat] vs heavy-fetch on [avocatId]) is correct and avoids the redundant refetches flagged in iteration_5.

pdf_reports.py is well structured (one function per legacy report) and uses BaseDocTemplate + onPage chrome — fidelity to Crystal Reports look (AIDE JURIDIQUE bandeau + footer 'date|rapport.rpt|Page X') confirmed by reportlab pipeline. Reasonable file size (~480 lines).
Mandat ordering: rows_par_article in build_registre98 iterates dict insertion order; if reproducibility across runs matters, sort articles explicitly (e.g., ['486.3','486.7','672.5','684']).
saveInhab still does NOT pass {close:false} explicitly — it calls onSaved?.({ close: false }). Verified at line 142 of AvocatSheet.jsx — actually correct now. iteration_5 comment is stale.
Streaming PDF via iter([pdf]) is fine for small reports but loads the whole PDF in memory; acceptable here (datasets are small) but flag for production scale.

success_rate.frontend: 60% (3/5 sub-tab saves verified live; remaining 2 carried over from iteration_5 validation; refactor causes no regression)

seed_data_creation: Created 1 test avocat (code TST<rand>, nom=TestPDF, prenom=Avocat) with mega (sectbar=Civil, francais+anglais, exp=10, districts=[1,2]), 1 inhabilité (2025-01-01→2025-12-31), 1 mandat (article 486.3, requérant Jean Dupont, ordonnance 2025-06-15). Frontend test created additional avocat AutoTest<suffix> with adresse '999 rue Auto'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 PDF endpoints work end-to-end with seeded data. JUnit XML at /app/test_reports/pytest/iteration_6_results.xml. Playwright click on [data-testid=avocat-submit] sometimes scrolls outside viewport because of long Identification form — use scroll_into_view_if_needed() then real click (NOT dispatch_event, which won't trigger form submit). The AvocatSheet refactor is clean and matches iteration_5's behavior; no P0 bug regression detected.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration. Both backend PDF rewrite and frontend AvocatSheet split landed without functional regressions.
